html:
RECOUP Sleep and Circadian Rhythm Clinic (RSCRC)

Sleep Disorders in India: An Urgent Health Concern

Did You Know? India is facing a significant rise in sleep disorders, especially in the wake of the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic. Recent studies reveal alarming prevalence rates of insomnia, obstructive sleep apnea (OSA), and restless legs syndrome (RLS) among various populations.

Key Statistics:

  • Insomnia: 25.7% of the population
  • Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA): 37.4%
  • Restless Legs Syndrome (RLS): 10.6%

Higher Risk Groups:

  • Individuals with Chronic Conditions: Increased rates of insomnia and OSA in those with diabetes and heart disease.
  • Healthy Population:
    • Insomnia: 15.1%
    • OSA: 14.6%

Alarming Findings:

  • Excessive Daytime Sleepiness: A staggering 19.6% of healthy individuals report excessive daytime sleepiness, highlighting the need for intervention.

Health Implications of Sleep Disorders:

Sleep disorders can lead to a range of serious health issues, including:

  • Cardiovascular Diseases: Increased risk of hypertension and heart disease.
  • Metabolic Disorders: Greater likelihood of obesity and type 2 diabetes.
  • Mental Health Issues: Higher incidence of anxiety and depression.
  • Impaired Immune Function: Increased susceptibility to infections.

Limitations of Conventional Approaches

While conventional approaches to treating insomnia often include prescription medications or over-the-counter sleep aids, these methods can come with limitations. Many individuals experience side effects, dependency issues, or short-term relief without addressing the underlying causes of their sleep disturbances. As a result, a more holistic and comprehensive approach is necessary to achieve lasting change.

RECOUP Interdisciplinary 4R Protocol

At RECOUP, we utilize a unique 4R Protocol designed to address insomnia from multiple angles:

R1: Relief

Our priority is to provide immediate relief from sleeplessness through personalized strategies, including:

  • Relaxation Techniques: Mindfulness practices and breathing exercises to calm the mind before bed.
  • Supplements
  • Adjuncts to promote sleep
  • Sleep hygiene training

R2: Root Causes

To achieve lasting change, we delve deeper to understand the root causes of your insomnia:

  • Comprehensive Assessments: Our Lifestyle Medicine/ Functional Medicine Physicians and Health Coaches evaluate your medical history, lifestyle factors, and sleep patterns through detailed questionnaires and sleep studies through wearable devices.
  • Identifying Underlying Issues: We investigate stress, anxiety, emotional trauma and other medical conditions that may impact your sleep quality.

R3: Rebalance

With the root causes identified, our team collaborates to rebalance your system:

  • Lifestyle Medicine/Functional Medicine Physicians: Conduct thorough evaluations and correct physiological imbalances that lead to sleep disturbances, such as improper sugar control, stress, obesity, and gastroesophageal reflux. Devices for OSA are prescribed.
  • Health Coaches: Help educate on sleep hygiene practices, set achievable goals and provide ongoing support.
  • Physiotherapists: Address physical issues that disrupt sleep through tailored exercises, Neurofeedback and other techniques.
  • Yoga Therapists: Develop customized yoga programs, including gentle poses, Yoga Nidra, and breathwork techniques.
  • Clinical Psychologists: Hypnotherapy, Nightmare Management, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (iCBT), and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (iACT) can be utilized to manage the cognitive and behavioral aspects of sleep.
  • Ayurveda Physicians: Offer holistic approaches, including dietary adjustments, CBD and herbal remedies.
  • Nutritionists: provide dietary assessments and recommendations to promote better sleep and weight management.

R4: Regeneration

We emphasize regeneration to ensure that improvements are sustainable:

  • Building Healthy Habits: Our team will guide you in integrating positive changes into your daily routine, fostering lasting impacts on your sleep quality.

The uniqueness of the RECOUP Sleep Clinic

The RECOUP Sleep and Circadian Rhythm Clinic offers a unique spectrum of care that integrates the latest advances in sleep science and holistic health. We focus on:

  • Understanding Sleep Disorders: By examining the complex interplay between sleep and various biological, psychological, and environmental factors, we aim to decode the intricacies of sleep disorders and their impact on overall health.
  • Personalized Interventions: We provide targeted clinical interventions that optimize sleep patterns, focusing on lifestyle modifications, cognitive-behavioral techniques, and physiological assessments to enhance restorative sleep.
  • Holistic Assessments: Our comprehensive sleep assessments evaluate not just the quantity of sleep but also its quality, cognitive function, hormonal influences, and environmental factors, ensuring a thorough understanding of each patient's unique sleep profile.
  • Building Resilience: We empower individuals to build physiological resilience against sleep disturbances through education, personalized care plans, and ongoing support, promoting sustainable improvements in sleep health.
  • Preventing Sleep-Related Health Issues: By identifying and addressing preventable sleep issues, we aim to mitigate the risk of chronic conditions associated with poor sleep, enhancing overall well-being across all age groups.

Comprehensive Sleep Assessment Tracks

To support our holistic approach, we have developed specialized assessment tracks for evaluating sleep health:

  • Sleep Quality Index: A comprehensive analysis of sleep duration, efficiency, and disturbances through sleep diaries, questionnaires and wearable technology.
  • Cognitive Function Index: A 24-parameter assessment evaluating cognitive functions related to sleep, including memory, attention, and executive function.
  • Hormonal Assessment: A multidimensional evaluation of hormones such as cortisol and melatonin to understand their impact on sleep patterns.
  • Circadian Rhythm Evaluation: Assessment of your circadian rhythms to identify misalignments that affect sleep quality and daily functioning.
  • Physical Function Assessment: Evaluation of physical activities and exercises that impact sleep, ensuring your body is well-prepared for restorative rest.
  • Psychological Assessment: Identifying cognitive and emotional factors, such as anxiety and depression, that contribute to sleep disturbances.
  • Environmental Assessment: Analysis of sleep environments, including light, noise, and temperature factors that may disrupt sleep.
  • Nutritional Assessment: Evaluating dietary habits and nutrient intake that may influence sleep quality and recommending improvements.

Who Would Benefit from Attending RSCRC?

Patients who typically visit our sleep clinic include individuals experiencing various sleep-related issues or suspected sleep disorders. Common reasons for seeking evaluation and treatment at RSCRC include:

  1. Suspected Sleep Apnea
  2. Insomnia
  3. Narcolepsy
  4. Parasomnias
  5. Restless Legs Syndrome (RLS)
  6. Shift Work Sleep Disorder
  7. Periodic Limb Movement Disorder (PLMD)
  8. Behavioral Sleep Disorders
  9. Evaluation of Daytime Sleepiness
  10. Snoring and Sleep-Related Breathing Disorders

Management Steps at RSCRC

Upon evaluation, our interdisciplinary team will develop a tailored treatment plan, which may include:

  • Lifestyle Modifications: Educating patients on sleep hygiene, regular sleep schedules, and relaxation techniques.
  • Behavioral Therapy: Utilizing Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I) to address negative thoughts and behaviors related to sleep.
  • Medications: Prescribing sleep aids or medications where appropriate to manage symptoms.
  • Continuous Positive Airway Pressure (CPAP): For patients with diagnosed sleep apnea, CPAP therapy may be recommended to maintain open airways during sleep.
  • Oral Appliances: Custom-fitted devices to help keep the airway open for those who prefer an alternative to CPAP.
  • Follow-up Assessments: Regular check-ins to monitor progress, adjust treatment plans, and ensure patient accountability.

RSCRC Team

Our dedicated RSCRC team includes:

  • Functional Medicine Physicians
  • Lifestyle Medicine Physicians
  • Rehabilitation Physicians
  • Other Specialist Physicians
  • Ayurveda Physicians
  • Naturopathy and Yoga Physicians
  • Nutritionists
  • Clinical Psychologists
  • Physiotherapists
  • Occupational Therapists
  • Aquatic Therapists
  • Health Coaches
  • Nurses
  • Other professionals, as required
  • Patient Care Coordinators

Self-Enrollment Form

Take the first step towards improved sleep and well-being. Contact us today to self-enroll or for more information on our services!

At the RECOUP Sleep and Circadian Rhythm Clinic, our interdisciplinary team is committed to providing personalized, holistic care that addresses your unique sleep needs. Ready to reclaim your sleep? Schedule your consultation with us today!
